title: Is SoundHound Stock a Buy Based on Its $140 Billion Total Addressable Market?
text: SoundHound delivered Q1 2026 revenue of $44.2 million, representing a 52% increase from the previous year. The company maintained its full-year guidance at $225-$260 million while projecting 2027 revenues of at least $350-$400 million. The company identifies a massive addressable market spanning multiple sectors, including smart devices, customer service, and retail. Real-world implementations show measurable results, with clients experiencing an 11% increase in revenue compared to non-AI locations. The recent stock decline following earnings may present buying opportunities for long-term investors. The article notes that SoundHound is a growth stock that doesn't have any debt, which is rare. However, prospective investors should expect volatility—the stock carries a beta of 2.7, making it more than twice as volatile as the broader markets. Market skepticism centers on expanding losses rather than operational execution, as the company continues securing marquee partnerships like Walmart.
